channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Key disaster loan program 'quickly exhausting' funds after hurricanes
item.description: Officials are warning a key federal program that provides disaster loans to businesses and homeowners is running critically low on funds in the aftermath of back-to-back hurricanes, increasing the pressure on Congress to return to Washington.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Fight for House raging in districts ringing NYC where Trump struggled but other Republicans thrived
item.description: Voters in a ring of congressional districts encircling New York City where Republican candidates often do well but Donald Trump struggled in 2020 could decide which party controls the U.S. House for the next two years.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Should California's minimum wage be $18? Voters will soon decide
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Voters will decide in November whether California should raise its hourly minimum wage to $18 by 2026 and pay workers what would be the highest statewide minimum wage in the country.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ted Cruz and Colin Allred wage another big US Senate fight in Texas
item.description: FORT WORTH, Texas (AP) — Attack ads on every TV break. Campaign money pouring in. And on a sunny Saturday, a crowd stretching out the door for a campaign rally at Tulip's, a popular Fort Worth nightclub — this time for Democratic U.S. Rep.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY and JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rebuilding Florida after Hurricane Milton and protecting against scams
item.description:
Recovery efforts can unite communities after catastrophic damage. Neighbors come together to help one another, while state officials and law enforcement work diligently to restore order. Gradually, residents begin to see their homes return to normalcy.
But even with the good, comes the bad.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DOJ sues Virginia for allegedly purging noncitizens from voting rolls too close to election
item.description: The Department of Justice is suing Virginia over its voter roll maintenance program, alleging that the state illegally removed voters too close to the election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Allred pushes back on Cruz transgender attack ads
item.description: “Ted Cruz is lying again, but now he's lying about our children. I'm a dad. I'm also a Christian,” Allred said on camera.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maria Torres-Springer discusses new role as NYC’s first deputy mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams on Tuesday appointed Maria Torres-Springer to be New York City’s new first deputy mayor after Sheena Wright resigned from her position.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: What is California Proposition 33?
item.description: California voters this November will decide whether to expand rent control statewide by repealing a 1995 law. The Costa-Hawkins Rental Housing Act currently limits a local government's ability to cap rents on newer properties and single-family homes.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Are male voters reluctant to vote for a woman? Harris' backers are confronting the question head on
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The concern has been there all along, but now it's being talked about openly: Are some men reluctant to vote for Democrat Kamala Harris because she’s a woman? The vice president rarely references her gender on the campaign trail, but her key supporters are starting to make more dir
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mayorkas grabs high-end sushi from DC Nobu directly after quick stop in Hurricane Helene-hit North Carolina
item.description: DHS Secretary Mayorkas was caught on camera at ritzy restaurant Nobu in D.C. just hours after visiting Hurricane Helene-torn North Carolina on official relief business.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: JD Vance refused five times to acknowledge Donald Trump lost 2020 election in podcast interview
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — JD Vance, Republican vice presidential nominee, again refused to acknowledge that President Joe Biden won the 2020 election over former President Donald Trump, evading the question five times in an interview with The New York Times, the newspaper reported Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Doug Emhoff doesn't deny report he slapped ex-girlfriend outside overseas movie event
item.description: Second gentleman Doug Emhoff called salacious allegations against him a "distraction" after a Daily Mail report claimed he once struck an ex-girlfriend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Republican demands answers as billions in FEMA relief are still going to COVID: 'Legitimate concern'
item.description: Rep. Chip Roy, R-Texas, sent a scathing letter to FEMA chief Deanne Criswell demanding answers on disaster relief immediate-use funding going to COVID concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris viewed more positively by Hispanic women than by Hispanic men: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A solid majority of Hispanic women have a positive opinion of Vice President Kamala Harris and a negative view of former President Donald Trump, but Hispanic men are more divided on both candidates, according to a recent poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate candidates steer clear of debates
item.description: Senate candidates this cycle are largely steering clear of the debate stage, in some cases avoiding it altogether, marking a major change from past years as campaigns question the debates' importance. For years, it was common for top Senate races to feature multiple debates.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Obama urges Black men to show up for Harris as he campaigns in critical Pennsylvania
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Former President Barack Obama gave a blistering critique of his White House successor Donald Trump and urged Black men to show up for Kamala Harris as he opened a swing-state tour for the Democratic ticket.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hunter Biden legal saga is ‘real war’ that 'preoccupied' outgoing president, new Woodward book claims
item.description: A new book by Bob Woodward details the emotional turmoil President Biden faced over his son Hunter's legal controversies, weighing heavily on his decision to exit the race.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate candidates steer clear of debates
item.description: Senate candidates this cycle are largely steering clear of the debate stage, in some cases avoiding it altogether, marking a major change from past years as campaigns question the debates' importance. For years, it was common for top Senate races to feature multiple debates.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's complaints about '60 Minutes' put a spotlight on editing at the nation's top newsmagazine
item.description: Donald Trump skipped a “60 Minutes” interview this past week, but he's attacking CBS for how it handled its session with opponent Kamala Harris, calling it a “giant fake news scam” and suggesting the network was out to protect her.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Speculation continues over Cuomo run for NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- There has been growing speculation this week that former Gov. Andrew Cuomo is rapidly approaching a decision about whether or not to run for New York City mayor.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Republican NY-18 candidate Alison Esposito says America doesn’t have a gun problem
item.description: “We have a heart problem in this country and a mental health problem in this country. We don’t have a gun problem in this country,” Esposito said.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota’s ‘uncommitted’ voters go into election unsatisfied over lack of cease-fire, conflicted over Harris
item.description: Minnesota sent the most “uncommitted” delegates to the Democratic National Convention in protest of President Joe Biden’s stance toward Israeli’s military actions in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump campaigns in 'make or break' Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Trump barnstormed Pennsylvania on Wednesday as he and his campaign have made clear the commonwealth could make or break his chances of winning in November. Trump held rallies in President Biden’s hometown of Scranton and in Reading.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: The presidential campaigns have made over 200 visits but speak to just a fraction of American voters
item.description: The nation's unique Electoral College system for electing a president, which replaces the popular vote, puts disproportionate voting power in the hands of a relative few states.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: The disconnect between economic data and voters' concerns
item.description: Are you worried about money? Concerned about the economy? MPR News host Angela Davis and her guests talk about the financial issues affecting how people plan to vote.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: Trump campaign blasts Bob Woodward; denies secret Putin calls
item.description: Donald Trump's campaign is rejecting claims in Bob Woodward's new book 'War' about the former president holding secret calls with Vladimir Putin since leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis pushes back on 'delusional' Harris remark about Hurricane Milton calls
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) said Monday that it was “delusional” for Vice President Kamala Harris to accuse him of “political gamesmanship” after reports emerged that he did not take the Democratic presidential nominee’s calls as his state braces for Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harris reveals owning a Glock: 'Of course' I've fired it
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris said in a "60 Minutes" interview that aired Monday that she owns a Glock and has fired it at a shooting range in another example of how she and her campaign have made a point to highlight her gun ownership.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Harris is pressed for more specifics in a wide-ranging interview with '60 Minutes'
item.description: The interview followed criticism from former President Donald Trump that Kamala Harris has been unwilling to sit for hard interviews. She was pressed on everything from her economic proposals to immigration.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Rafael Arroyo discusses wasteful spending, studio tax credits, and California's policies in Nevada
item.description: Rafael Arroyo, the Republican candidate running for Assembly District 41, joined Brian Loftis on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide, outlining his vision and priorities as the election approaches.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How Texas’ environmental agency weakened a once-rigorous air pollution monitoring team
item.description: Former employees say the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality gutted the unit soon after the fracking boom swept the state oil industry. The operation never returned to what it was before.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Dylan Baddour And Peter Aldhous, Inside Climate News, KPRC 2

item.title: Texas students with immigrant parents lost out on college aid because of FAFSA glitch
item.description: The glitch kept the students from submitting the form on time, which put them last in line to receive aid this year.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Sneha Dey, KPRC 2
